description: All wood flooring installed, acclimated, finished, and inspected to close tolerances before final punch and client walkthrough.

items.label: Flooring species, grade, width, and finish confirmed against client selection sheet — no substitutions without signed CO
Subfloor moisture reading taken and logged: wood subfloor must be ≤12% MC; concrete slab must be ≤75% RH per ASTM F2170 or ≤3 lbs/1000 sf/24hr per calcium chloride test
Subfloor flatness verified: no more than 3/16" gap under 10-foot straightedge per NWFA installation guidelines
Subfloor fastened at all low/soft spots — squeaks and bounce eliminated before flooring goes down
All HVAC, plumbing, and electrical rough-ins complete; no scheduled penetrations through finished floor areas
HVAC system running and interior temperature and humidity stabilized (65–75°F, 35–55% RH) for minimum 5 days before acclimation begins
Painter's work in flooring zones complete or confirmed not to return with heavy foot traffic, rolling ladders, or water-based products
Flooring delivered and acclimation start date logged; species-appropriate acclimation period documented (minimum 3 days, manufacturer or NWFA requirement confirmed)
Flooring stored flat, off slab, on stickers or pallets; shrink wrap opened to allow air circulation
Acclimation moisture readings taken on arrival and again at install — delta between flooring MC and subfloor MC within 2% for solid hardwood
All flooring bundles inspected for milling defects, color variation, and finish inconsistencies before installation begins; culls pulled and supplier notified if quantity-significant
Sufficient overage on site to complete run (minimum 7–10% waste factor confirmed against order quantity)
Layout direction confirmed with superintendent and client (typically parallel to longest exterior wall or primary sight line); chalk line snapped and verified square to room
Appropriate moisture barrier or underlayment installed per manufacturer spec and subfloor type (e.g., 15 lb felt on wood subfloor, 6-mil poly on concrete)
First three rows blind-nailed and face-nailed; remaining field rows blind-nailed with flooring nailer at 6–8" spacing or per manufacturer spec
Expansion gap maintained at all vertical obstructions — walls, cabinets, islands — minimum 3/4" or per manufacturer spec; not covered by base that is glued to floor
Transitions (reducers, T-moldings, thresholds) roughed in at doorways and room changes; species and finish match confirmed before ordering
Stair nosings or feature inlays (if specified) installed per design drawings; miter joints tight, no gaps visible
Nail or staple schedule consistent throughout; no surface dimples, hammer strikes, or skip-nailing in field
Rough sanding complete with drum sander (36–60 grit as needed); edges addressed with edge sander; no chatter marks or swirl patterns in field
Final sand sequence completed through 80–100–120 grit; surface uniformly smooth with no visible scratches under raking light inspection
Stain sample approved on actual installed flooring in-place by client (written or text/email approval on file) before full coat applied
Finish coats applied per manufacturer spec — product name, sheen level, and number of coats documented in project file
Re-coat scuff-sand between finish coats; no dust nibs, bubbles, lap marks, or fish-eyes visible in cured finish
Floors sealed off 24–48 hours between coats; no foot traffic until final coat fully cured per manufacturer specification
Full floor inspected under raking light for: hollow spots, loose boards, squeaks, lippage exceeding 1/32" between adjacent boards, open seams exceeding 1/16" (NWFA tolerance for site-finished)
All squeaks and hollow spots remedied before base molding is installed
Finish consistency uniform across entire floor — no sheen variation, lap lines, or unfinished edges at room perimeter
Expansion gaps at walls confirmed present and will be covered by base/shoe without fastening base to flooring
Transitions and thresholds fully installed, fastened, and flush; no trip hazards at any room change
Floor protection (Ram Board or equivalent) installed and taped in all traffic lanes immediately after final cure; protection to remain until 10 days before client walkthrough
Superintendent final walk of all wood flooring zones completed and punch items resolved
Client walkthrough of wood flooring completed; client signs or approves in writing that flooring meets selections and workmanship expectations
Flooring installer's warranty documentation (product and labor) collected and filed in project folder
Maintenance instructions (cleaning products, humidity range, touch-up kit) provided to client in homeowner manual
Any warranty claims or open punch items documented in Cost Tracker with responsible party and resolution date assigned
